channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump signs tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China
item.description: "Tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China are SIGNED!" White House spokesperson Harrison Fields posted on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to settle with woman who says ex-Officer Chauvin knelt on her neck
item.description: The city of Minneapolis has agreed to pay $600,000 to settle a lawsuit by a woman who alleged that ex-Officer Derek Chauvin hauled her from her minivan and pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just as he did four months later when he killed George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Recovery crews return to the Potomac debris after the deadly plane and helicopter crash
item.description: Recovery crews and divers searched the Potomac River for remains and cleared wreckage Saturday from the midair collision of a passenger jet and Army helicopter that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ken Martin elected next DNC chair
item.description: Minnesota state party chair Ken Martin was elected on Saturday as the next leader of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) after securing a majority of the 448 DNC committee members’ votes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Groundhog Day 2025: What to know
item.description: Will spring arrive early? We will find out Sunday whether the groundhog Punxsutawney Phil sees his shadow or not.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Author's story of coping after a wildfire resonates with community affected by latest LA-area fires
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — On a quiet summer evening in June 1990, Pico Iyer sat in his family home in Santa Barbara, California, when suddenly, he was surrounded by walls of flames five stories high.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palisades, Eaton fires in Southern California 100% contained, officials say
item.description: The Palisades Fire and Eaton Fire that burned thousands of acres in the Los Angeles area have been 100% contained, fire officials said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: court records
item.description: A settlement has been reached in the lawsuit involving an Arkansas-based couple and stars of HGTV's "Fixer to Fabulous" just days before the parties were set to go to trial.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KTLA 5

item.title: Beneath the surface, Groundhog Day's deep roots predate Punxsutawney Phil
item.description: Groundhog Day predictions about the coming of spring put the spotlight on Punxsutawney Phil every Feb. 2, but celebrating the midpoint between the shortest and darkest day of the year and the spring equinox has ancient roots in European agricultural life.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An air ambulance has crashed in Philadelphia. What to know about air medical transport
item.description: A medical transport jet carrying a child patient, her mother and four others slammed into a Philadelphia neighborhood and exploded in flames.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Carolina puts inmate Marion Bowman Jr. to death in state’s third execution since September
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina put a third inmate to death in four months Friday as it goes through a backlog of prisoners who exhausted their appeals while the state couldn’t find lethal injection drugs. Marion Bowman Jr. was executed at 6:27 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Air traffic controllers union responds to Trump's DEI attacks
item.description: The air traffic controllers union responded Friday to claims by President Trump this week that diversity programs contributed to the fatal crash in Washington Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, Fox 5

item.title: CBS agrees to hand over '60 Minutes' Harris interview transcripts to FCC
item.description: CBS says it will turn over unedited transcripts of its October interview with Kamala Harris to the Federal Communications Commission.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about the NTSB, the agency investigating the DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people brought renewed focus on the federal agency charged with investigating aviation disasters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York doctor indicted for allegedly prescribing abortion pill to patient via telemedicine in Louisiana
item.description: A female New York doctor was recently indicted on felony charges for allegedly prescribing abortion medication to a patient via telemedicine in Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tour guide at former Colorado gold mine fell out of elevator after apparently not latching door
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A tour guide killed at a former Colorado gold mine last year fell out of a crowded elevator taking tourists below ground after apparently not latching its door closed, documents obtained by The Associated Press show.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Delphi murders: Convicted killer in hiking trail slayings cites new evidence in bid to overturn conviction
item.description: Delphi murders suspect Richard Allen, found guilty of brutally murdering two teen girls on an Indiana hiking trail in 2017, is asking a Carroll County court to overturn his conviction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man rams cars while 'upset' over Nashville traffic: Police
item.description: Police say he was "apparently upset" about the congested downtown traffic after the Nashville Predators game.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Guerry, NewsNation

item.title: 40+ bodies pulled from Potomac near Reagan National
item.description: More than 40 bodies have been recovered from the Potomac River after a deadly mid-air collision sent an Army Black Hawk helicopter and American Airlines plane diving into the icy water earlier this week, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: PETA issues swap deal: Punxsutawney Phil for a vegan cake
item.description: PETA is urging Punxsutawney Groundhog Club to remove Punxsutawney Phil from his Groundhog Day duties.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Flight recorders recovered in DCA crash
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) said Thursday that they recovered flight data recorders, known as black boxes, from the aircraft involved in the deadly mid-air collision near Reagan Washington National Airport earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says helicopter in crash was flying too high
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday asserted the military helicopter involved in a deadly midair collision near Washington, D.C., was "flying too high.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump aid freeze disrupts global HIV/AIDS fight
item.description: A legacy bipartisan initiative to combat HIV and AIDS in Africa is collateral damage from President Donald Trump’s directive to halt all U.S. foreign assistance, despite efforts to exempt humanitarian assistance and lifesaving medication from being caught up in the three-month funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Congress authorized more flights at Reagan National despite warnings
item.description: Congress added more flights to Reagan National's schedule last year despite warnings from the airport.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump makes his first moves on education, but no McMahon hearing in sight
item.description: President Trump made his first major moves on education Wednesday, signing a series of executive orders that come even before his nominee to lead the Education Department, Linda McMahon, has had her Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump warns Canada, Mexico tariffs are coming on Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday said he plans to follow through on Saturday on his threat to impose 25 percent tariffs on goods from Mexico and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pioneering Black Army general who lived in El Paso dies at 100
item.description: Retired Army Maj. Gen. Edward Greer, who broke racial barriers during his long Army career, died at age 100 at his El Paso home on Wednesday, Jan. 29, his family said.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Midair collision kills 67 people in the deadliest US air disaster in almost a quarter century
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — A midair collision between an Army helicopter and a jetliner killed all 67 people aboard the two aircraft, officials said Thursday, as they scrutinized the actions of the military pilot in the country's deadliest aviation disaster in almost a quarter century.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans leave Florida frustrated with lack of final plan to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) arrived at this week’s House Republican retreat with hopes of uniting the fractious GOP conference around a plan to pass President Trump's agenda — but instead, the group is departing south Florida with rising tensions in its ranks. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: White House aide says spending freeze isn't being rescinded despite memo
item.description: The White House is claiming the Wednesday move by the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) rescinding a controversial order that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance is not actually an end to curbing government spending. “This is NOT a rescission of the federal funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri woman pardoned for Jan. 6 offenses sentenced in deadly drunken-driving crash
item.description: Emily Hernandez, a Missouri woman pardoned after her ties to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol, was sentenced Wednesday in connection with a drunken-driving crash.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: Former Pennsylvania native, family killed in DC plane crash with army helicopter
item.description: JOHNSTOWN, Pa. (WTAJ) — A former Central Pennsylvania native along with her husband and two kids are among those who died in a plane crash Wednesday night when it collided with an army helicopter near Washington D.C.
item.dc:creator: Rian Bossler, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In ‘2000 Meters to Andriivka,’ Oscar winner takes viewers back to Ukraine’s front lines
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — The day Mstyslav Chernov won the BAFTA for his documentary “20 Days in Mariupol” was the day he learned two soldiers he knew had been killed in combat.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here are some of the deadliest plane crashes in US history
item.description: An American Airlines jet with 60 passengers and four crew members aboard collided Wednesday with an Army helicopter while coming in for a landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington, D.C. Everyone aboard was feared dead, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Passenger jet with 64 aboard collides with Army helicopter while landing at Reagan Airport near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — An American Airlines jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members collided Wednesday with an Army helicopter while landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington, prompting a large search-and-rescue operation in the nearby Potomac River.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former US Sen. Bob Menendez gets 11 years in prison for taking bribes and acting as agent of Egypt
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez was sentenced Wednesday to 11 years in prison for accepting bribes of gold and cash and acting as an agent of Egypt — crimes his lawyer said he’s been mocked for as “Gold Bar Bob.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, GEOFF MULVIHILL and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4
